[2026-06-14 16:30 EST – 2026-06-15 06:00 EST]

🔥 Top Stories
• U.S.-Iran peace deal / MOU reached — Iran says ceasefire begins immediately; signing set for June 19 in Geneva/Switzerland, with 60-day nuclear talks to follow.
• Hormuz to reopen; blockade easing — Trump says Strait of Hormuz reopens Friday as U.S. naval blockade lifts, a major relief for energy/shipping.
• Oil plunges, risk assets rally — Brent and U.S. crude fell sharply; S&P futures, global equities, AUD/NZD, and Asian stocks jumped on de-escalation hopes.

⛽ Oil & Energy
• Crude hits sub-$80/bbl — U.S. crude fell below $80 for first time since March; Brent dropped ~4-5% on peace-deal headlines.
• Hormuz shipping restart in focus — LNG tanker Disha transited Hormuz; shipowners remain cautious over mines/implementation risk.
• Energy price shock unwinds — ECB says oil shock may still take months to normalize, even as immediate geopolitical premium fades.

📊 Markets & Macro
• Global “risk-on” surge — Nikkei surged above 69,000, ASX/KOSPI/Taiwan/Indonesia rallied, and Europe futures/ STOXX 600 hit fresh highs.
• Rates move lower — U.S. 10Y Treasury yield fell to one-month low; German JGBs and eurozone yields eased on lower geopolitical risk.
• Dollar softens; FX shifts — USD weakened broadly while AUD, CAD and Asian FX strengthened on fading safe-haven demand.
• Gold still bid — Gold hit record highs above $4,300/oz despite the risk rally, showing lingering hedging demand.

🌍 Geopolitical
• Middle East ceasefire framework — Pakistan, Qatar, Egypt, Saudi Arabia, Kuwait, UAE, UK, EU and Japan all welcomed the deal and urged durable peace.
• Implementation remains fragile — Iran, Israel and regional officials keep signaling unresolved conditions, including Lebanon, sanctions and nuclear verification.
• Ukraine war intensifies — Russia launched major overnight strikes on Kyiv/Kharkiv; Poland scrambled jets as border tensions rose.

🪙 Crypto & DeFi
• Bitcoin rebounds — BTC reclaimed $65k as the peace deal lifted risk appetite.
• Shorts squeezed — Roughly $150M in crypto shorts were liquidated after the Iran headline.

🏛️ Regulatory & Policy
• Sanctions relief tied to verification — E4/UK say sanctions could be lifted only after clear, verifiable nuclear steps from Iran.
• Fed/ECB watch — ECB officials still warn of upside inflation risks and say policy remains data-dependent.
• Trade tensions linger — Trump threatened France with 100% wine tariffs over tech tax; India said U.S./UK trade talks continue.

🏢 Corporate
• AI/data center trade still hot — Schneider-Foxconn alliance targets AI data centers; Anthropic/Bytedance chip and GPU demand stories kept AI names active.
• Megacaps/discretionary moves — SpaceX cited at $1T revenue target by 2030; Adobe target cut by Citi.
• Industrial/healthcare updates — AstraZeneca priority review in U.S.; J&J announced $1B+ Jacksonville investment.
